meta.title: Brothers Hockey Training Program
meta.subtitle: 10-week summer program — goalie (almost 10) + skater (12)
meta.schedule: 3 sessions/week, rest day between each. 1 Field Day + 2 Basement Days per week, ordered flexibly based on weather. Odd weeks use V1 variants where present, even weeks use V2.

legend.SS: Superset — exercises run as A then B back-to-back (or as partners: one does A while the other does B, then swap), with rest after completing both.
legend.split: Position split — goalie (G) and skater (S) each do a different exercise in this slot.
legend.G/S specs: Where reps/weights differ by athlete, both are given.
legend.game: End-of-session game finisher, chosen from the games menu by the boys (alternating who picks).

games.name: Mini shootout
games.desc: Skater shoots a tennis/soft ball at a taped goal or net, goalie defends. Works indoors against a wall or outdoors with a real net. Counts as position training for both. Track saves vs goals.

games.name: Save streak
games.desc: Dad tosses soft balls at the goalie at varying heights/corners — count consecutive saves. Skater version: consecutive one-timer catch-and-return against a wall.

games.name: Deck of cards
games.desc: Draw a card: hearts = squat jumps, diamonds = dot hops, clubs = shuffles, spades = push-ups. Number = reps. Brothers alternate draws for 3 minutes.

games.name: Mirror battle
games.desc: Brothers face each other. One leads shuffles/fakes/drops for 30 sec, the other mirrors. Swap. Dad judges who tracked better — loser does 5 burpees.

games.name: Beat the timer
games.desc: 60 seconds: max lateral shuffles between lines 3m apart. Each brother chases his own record — separate fridge chart per kid.

games.name: Animal relays
games.desc: Bear crawl down, frog jump back, crab walk down, sprint back. Brothers race head-to-head, or both race Dad. Best on the field where there's room to sprint at the end.

games.name: Red light, green light — hockey edition
games.desc: Green = sprint with full arm drive. Red: goalie drops to butterfly/lateral stance, skater snaps into a low hockey-stop stance. Caught moving = 3 steps back. Field-only for the sprint version; basement version shortens to shuffles.

games.name: Chase tag
games.desc: 10m head start, chaser tries to tag before the line. Needs the field — best use of open space. Nothing develops kid speed better than chasing and being chased.

tests.label: Test day — last session of each phase (weeks 3, 6, 10)
tests.rule: The handicap rule: both boys run the same six tests, but the competition is percent improvement from their own last test, not raw score. Two columns on the fridge chart, one trophy line: 'most improved this phase.'

items.name: Standing long jump
items.how: Best of 3 attempts, must stick the landing

items.name: Single-leg balance, eyes closed
items.how: Each leg, until a foot or hand touches down

items.name: Deep squat hold
items.how: Heels down, below parallel, hands forward

houseRules.title: Partner supersets
houseRules.body: One brother does exercise A while the other does B, then they swap. Dumbbells are never needed twice at once, nobody stands idle, and the rest timer starts when both finish the round.

houseRules.title: Growth spurt watch (12yo)
houseRules.body: If he is in or entering a growth spurt: flexibility work matters more (never skip cool-downs), and any recurring pain just below the kneecap or in the heel means cut jumping volume in half for that week — keep everything else.

houseRules.title: Weekly rhythm + vacation rule
houseRules.body: Mon/Wed/Fri-style spacing with rest days between. Odd weeks V1, even weeks V2. Short on time or traveling: 10-minute minimum = one superset of choice + one game + pigeon pose, both boys. Missed weeks extend the phase — never skip ahead to catch up.

houseRules.title: Rain swap rule
houseRules.body: The Field Day can be ANY of the week's 3 sessions — whichever day has good weather. If a planned Field Day gets rained out, run a Basement Day instead and shift Field Day to the next good-weather day, even mid-week. Across the week, still aim for 3 sessions total.

houseRules.title: Dad's role
houseRules.body: Caller for reaction drills, judge for mirror battle, rabbit in chase tag, shooter in save streak. Jumping into sprints/relays yourself is worth more than instruction — kids match the energy in the room.

cues: Knees track over the 2nd toe — never caving inward, especially on landing
Land soft: toes first, roll to heel, bent knees. Loud landings = bad mechanics
Chest stays up at the bottom — no rounding forward
Squat jumps.warn: Stop if knees cave on landing. Regress to regular squats until controlled. On a field, grass is much softer than concrete — still land soft.
Squat jumps.hockey: For the goalie it's the butterfly position; for the skater it's the loaded position before every stride.

cues: Lower back pressed firmly into the mat — zero gap, that's the whole exercise
Lower opposite arm and leg slowly (3-4 sec)
Breathe out as limbs lower
Dead bug.warn: If the back arches off the mat, the rep doesn't count. Reduce range.

cues: Stay low the entire time — hips never rise during the shuffle
if he pops up, reduce reps
Feet never cross — push off the trailing foot
On 'freeze': wide stable stance, weight centered, hands up like holding a stick
Lateral shuffle + freeze.warn: Feet crossing = slow down before adding speed.
Lateral shuffle + freeze.hockey: Post-to-post for the goalie; defensive-zone footwork for the skater.

cues: Ball at lower back, feet walked out so knees hit 90° with vertical shins
3-count down, 1-sec pause, normal speed up
Weight through heels throughout
Quick, springy contacts on the balls of the feet
Knees soft and slightly bent the whole time
Accuracy first, speed second — clean landings on each dot
Big arm swing back, then forcefully forward as he leaves the ground
Stick the landing 3 full seconds — wide feet, bent knees. Stumble = failed rep
Standing long jump.warn: Watch for landing with knees together or legs locking out straight on impact. Grass is forgiving — great surface for this one.
Standing long jump.hockey: 'Stick the landing' builds the balance needed when diving for a save (goalie) or absorbing contact (skater).

cues: Start in proper goalie stance — feet wide, knees bent, hands forward
React to the call, don't anticipate — vary your rhythm as the caller
Wide stable lunge each time, then recover to stance quickly
React & drop.warn: On hard floors, keep this a standing lateral lunge — no knee drops. Grass allows safer knee-down variations if desired.
React & drop.hockey: This IS the goalie's game movement. Every rep should feel like a real save attempt.

cues: Hips stay up high — don't let them sag to the ground
Hand and same-side foot move together
Stay low through every direction change
Touch each cone with the closest hand — forces a low position
Backpedal with short quick steps, weight slightly forward
Low squat throughout — no popping up between moves
Watch the partner's hips, not their feet — hips don't lie
Quick small steps beat big slow ones
Mirror drill.hockey: Goalie tracks the puck carrier; skater learns defensive mirroring. Works in either location.

cues: Green = sprint with good arm drive
Red: goalie drops to butterfly/lateral stance, skater snaps into a low hockey-stop stance
Caught moving on red = back 3 steps. Make it competitive
Red light, green light — hockey edition.hockey: Combines sprint mechanics with position-specific stance under a reaction demand — multiple skills in one game.

cues: Hold the straps for balance only — minimal arm tension
Lower as deep as possible — heels flat, knees pushed wide apart
Chest up, spine long — not hunching over the knees
TRX assisted deep squat hold.hockey: Goalie: butterfly depth. Skater: the deep knee bend every coach yells about. Goal over the summer is deeper and more comfortable.

cues: Forearm directly under shoulder, body straight head to heel
Push the hip up and hold — don't let it sag toward the floor
Can do from the knee to start — progress to full side plank
Sit tall — both hips and knees at roughly 90°, one shin in front, one behind
Front hip: lean forward over the front shin slowly, spine long
Back hip: sit upright and rotate chest toward the back knee
Don't force both hips flat immediately — the raised side lowers over weeks
Hip 90/90 stretch.warn: If one side is dramatically tighter, spend extra time there. Athletes are often asymmetrical from their dominant push-off side.

cues: Lower back stays in contact with the mat during the pass
Slow and controlled — no swinging the ball
Step wide — much wider than feels natural. The working hip needs to load deeply
Sit back and to the side into the bent hip — toes of both feet pointing forward
Straight leg stays long — should feel a stretch in the inner thigh
Push through the heel of the bent leg to return
Lateral lunge.hockey: Goalie: butterfly split. Skater: the deep edge-work position. Both positions need this range.

cues: Skip with a high knee drive — thigh to parallel
Opposite arm drives with the knee, elbows at 90°
Snap the foot down underneath the body, land on the ball of the foot
A-skips.hockey: Sprint school. Mastering the A-skip pattern is the fastest path to a faster kid. Real distance on grass makes this drill what it's meant to be.

cues: Stand tall, lean forward from the ankles like a plank until balance breaks
First steps are short, fast and behind the body — push the ground back
Stay low for the first 5m, rise gradually
Falling starts.hockey: Teaches acceleration posture — the lean and push that slow runners are usually missing.

cues: Short, quick dip before jumping — not a deep squat first
Land soft in a partial squat. Loud = box too high
Always step down, never jump down backward — protects the Achilles
Box jumps.warn: Knees caving on the landing = stop the set. For the 12yo: any pain below the kneecap = halve jump volume that week. Indoors, use a foam/rubber mat for noise and impact.
Box jumps.hockey: The dip-and-explode is the same fast-twitch response as pushing off the post (goalie) or first stride (skater).

cues: Body straight as a plank from heels to head — no sagging hips
Pull elbows back past the body, chest reaches the hands, squeeze shoulder blades
Lower slowly (2-3 sec) — the lowering phase builds most of the strength
Skater (S): walk feet further forward for a harder angle
Low athletic stance at the start — weight forward on balls of feet
Plant the outside foot hard on direction changes and push laterally — don't slow to a stop
Stay low through every cut — standing up loses 0.2-0.3 seconds
Touch the line with the closest hand — forces a low body position
5-10-5 shuttle run.hockey: Time every rep, separate records per athlete. Yard lines give exact 5-yard increments — ideal on a field.

cues: Standing leg has a soft bend at the knee — never locked straight
Reach forward slowly with full control — no swinging or momentum
Hip of the standing leg stays level — watch for the pelvis dropping
dropped hip = weak glute med
Single-leg balance + reach.warn: Falling repeatedly on the same side may mean one ankle is weaker — give it extra time.

cues: Use the falling-start lean to begin each one
Arms drive hard, hands hip-to-cheek
Full walk-back recovery between sprints — speed work needs full rest
Driveway sprints.hockey: On a field, use real yard-line distances (10-15m). Short maximal sprints with full recovery are how kids actually get faster.

cues: Push off the outside edge of one foot, land on the other
Stick each landing 1 full second before the next bound
Land with the knee over the toe — no inward collapse
Lateral bounds.warn: If landings collapse, shorten the bound distance.
Lateral bounds.hockey: For the skater this is the crossover and stride push as a jump — the most hockey-specific jump there is.

cues: Proper goalie stance to start every rep
Pivot and drop-step — don't shuffle first
Hold the wide lunge 2 full seconds
Drop-step to save.warn: On hard floors, keep this standing (lunge, not knee-drop).
Drop-step to save.hockey: This IS the goalie's post-to-post game movement.

cues: Set a line or low stick on the ground
Cross the outside leg over, push laterally off the back foot — like a crossover stride
Stay low, chest up, eyes forward the whole drill
Crossover step-overs.hockey: Skater-only: this is the crossover pattern off-ice. Drive off the outside edge of the back foot.

cues: Body straight from head to heels — no sagging hips
Lower chest to handle level — elbows at roughly 45° from the body
On the hop sideways: both hands move together — keep hips level during the hop
Cut off the outside foot at each cone
Stay low through the weave, sprint tall on the way back
Stay in a low ready stance throughout
Vary the throw height and angle each rep — unpredictability is the point
Catch with one hand where possible — trains each side independently
Wall ball reaction.hockey: Directly trains the anticipation and hand quickness a goalie needs; lacrosse ball adds difficulty.

cues: Start in a low athletic stance — weight on balls of feet, knees slightly bent
Eyes on the ball the moment it's released — react, don't anticipate
Dive or lunge with control — recover quickly to ready position after each catch
Reaction ball drops.hockey: Pure reaction training. Can also roll a ball unpredictably along the ground for variety.

cues: Hold dumbbell vertically at chest — elbows tucked in, not flared
Descend slowly (3 sec), pushing knees outward as you go down
cue: 'push your knees apart with your elbows'
Heels must stay flat on the floor — if they lift, widen the stance slightly
Drive through the whole foot — squeeze glutes at the top
Goblet squat.warn: Heels lifting is most common. Widen stance or use a small heel raise (book under heels) while mobility improves.
Goblet squat.hockey: Deep squat = goalie butterfly = skater stride depth. Same lesson, both boys: 'that's your butterfly/stride position.'

cues: Push hips back first — this is a hip hinge, not a squat
Keep dumbbells close to the legs the whole way down — almost brush the shins
Back stays flat throughout — no rounding of the lower back
cue: 'long neck, tall spine'
Stop when hamstrings pull — don't force range beyond tension
Drive hips forward to stand — squeeze glutes at the top
Romanian deadlift.warn: Lower back rounding is the key thing to catch on both boys. Reduce weight or limit range.
Romanian deadlift.hockey: Hamstrings and glutes are the skating engine — the most important lift in the program.

cues: Lie on back, feet flat, lower back gently pressed into the floor
Press straight up — wrists stacked over elbows at the bottom
Lower slowly (2-3 sec) — elbows at roughly 45° from the body
Start in a solid plank — hands under shoulders, core braced
Ball under the shins, not the feet — keeps it stable
Pull knees toward chest by curling the lower back — don't let hips shoot up
Slow and controlled both ways — not a speed exercise
Swiss ball jackknife.warn: If the ball rolls sideways, hips are rotating. 5 clean reps beats 10 sloppy ones.

cues: Upper back on the ball, hips up in a bridge, feet flat
Hold one light dumbbell with straight arms, rotate the shoulders side to side
Hips stay square and high — only the torso rotates
Swiss ball Russian twist.hockey: Skater-only: rotational power is shooting power. Slow and controlled before fast.

cues: Push through the heel of the stepping foot — not the toes
Drive the opposite knee up high at the top — pause 1 sec
Torso upright throughout — no leaning over the stepping leg
Step down with control — don't fall off the box
In push-up position, body straight from head to heels
Tap opposite shoulder alternating — keep hips completely still
If hips sway, slow down — control beats speed here
Front shin across the mat, hips square and level
Fold forward slowly — use forearms or a folded blanket under the front hip if needed
Breathe into the tightness — exhale and let the hip soften. Don't force it
6 slow breaths per side — a breathing game, not a clock-watch
Pigeon pose.warn: Sharp pain at the knee = adjust shin angle. Pain in the hip joint itself = stop.
Pigeon pose.hockey: The single most important stretch for the goalie; for the 12yo it guards against the flexibility loss that comes with growth spurts. Every session, no exceptions.

cues: Jump, land, absorb — then immediately bound sideways off one foot
Stick the single-leg landing 1 full second
Knee over toe on every landing
Combo: squat jump + lateral bound.warn: If the bound landing collapses, separate the two movements until each is solid. Grass is ideal — soft landings both directions.
Combo: squat jump + lateral bound.hockey: Goalie: post push + lateral drive. Skater: stride + crossover. Same drill, both positions.

cues: Lower until the free heel just taps the ground, then drive up
Knee of working leg tracks over the 2nd toe — watch from the front for caving
Hinge forward slightly at the hips — arms extend forward for balance
Start very low (4-6 inches). Height earns nothing if form breaks down
Single-leg squat to box.warn: Consistent knee caving = back to two-leg squats and more glute work. On a field without a box, use a soccer ball or low cone target.
Single-leg squat to box.hockey: Almost every save and every stride is a single-leg push.

cues: Lean into the resistance — drive the ground backward
Short powerful steps, arms pumping
Resisted sprint.hockey: TRX loose around the waist, Dad anchors the other end — works great on grass.

cues: Jog 5m to build, then hit max speed for 10m, then coast
Tall posture at top speed — eyes forward, relaxed face and hands
Full recovery between reps
Flying sprints.hockey: Max-speed exposure with relaxed form — top-end speed needs real distance, the field's best use.

cues: Raise to shoulder height only, slight elbow bend
3-sec lowering — that's where the work is
Swinging = too heavy
Dumbbell lateral raise.warn: In a low-ceiling basement, stop the raise at chest height instead of shoulder height.

cues: Sprint every arm of the star — no jogging
Touch each cone with the nearest hand
The push back to centre matters as much as the sprint out
5-cone star drill.warn: Needs real width — best on a field.
5-cone star drill.hockey: Multi-directional speed is what separates quick goalies and skaters from slow ones.

cues: Back foot in strap, ~12-16 inches off the ground
Lower straight down — front knee to 90°, back knee drops toward floor
Front knee stays stacked over the ankle — not past the toes
Torso upright — no leaning forward. Hands on hips helps
1-second pause at the bottom — don't skip it
TRX single-leg lunge.warn: Can't hold the bottom steady = do split squats on a bench first.

cues: Hold one dumbbell in both hands at one hip
Rotate diagonally up and across to the opposite shoulder — arms fairly straight
Power comes from the hips turning, not the arms — pivot the back foot
Standing dumbbell chop.warn: Low ceiling: stop the chop at chest height, don't take it overhead.
Standing dumbbell chop.hockey: Skater-only: the wrist shot and slap shot are hip rotation. This is shot power off-ice.

cues: Fast down, explosive up, no pause — bodyweight only
Depth stays honest even at speed — thighs to parallel
Weight at chest, elbows up and parallel to the floor — if elbows drop, weight lifts off
Descend slowly (3 sec) — knees push wide, chest stays tall
Drive straight up — don't let elbows drop on the way up
Heels flat on the floor throughout — widen stance if they lift
Front squat.warn: Elbows dropping = weight tips forward = lower back rounds. Reduce weight first.
Front squat.hockey: Front-loaded squat trains the upright posture both positions need.

cues: Knee and hand on bench — back flat and parallel to the floor
Pull elbow straight back toward the hip — not out to the side
Lower slowly (2-3 sec) — don't just drop the weight
No rotating the torso to help the lift — keep hips and shoulders square
Start standing, gripping TRX handles — body upright before you lean out
Walk feet back and lean the whole body forward as one rigid unit — arms extend overhead
Hips must not pike or sag — the body stays straight like a plank throughout
Pull back by engaging the lats — not by bending at the hips
TRX fallout.warn: If hips sag or pike, shorten the range of motion. Under a low ceiling, keep the range short.

weeks.name: Field Day — Sprint Foundations
weeks.tip: This can be ANY of the 3 weekly sessions — whichever day has field access and good weather. The other two are basement days, always available.
weeks.swap: Rained out? Run a Basement Day instead, and do this field session on the next good-weather day. Across the week you still get 3 sessions in — the order just shifts.

exercises.name: High knees + leg swings + arm circles
exercises.detail: 30 sec each, flowing

exercises.name: A-skips
exercises.detail: High knee drive, opposite arm, snap the foot down

items.note: Jumps on grass — soft landings, real distance

exercises.name: Standing long jump
exercises.detail: Max distance, stick the landing 3 sec — separate records

exercises.name: Crab walk race
exercises.detail: Sideways 5m and back, hips high

items.note: Position split — real space at last

g.name: React & drop
g.detail: Dad calls left/right — lunge into wide lateral stance, low and stable

s.name: Lateral bounds
s.detail: Bound side to side, stick each landing on grass

exercises.name: Falling starts
exercises.detail: Lean until balance breaks, then sprint 10m

blocks.label: Game finisher (4 min) — they alternate picking

exercises.name: Quad stretch + hip flexor lunge + child's pose
exercises.detail: 45-60 sec each — bring a towel for the grass

weeks.name: Basement Day A — Strength + Core
weeks.tip: This and Basement Day B are your rain-day fallbacks — always runnable regardless of weather.

exercises.name: Cat-cow + world's greatest stretch
exercises.detail: 10 cat-cows, 5 per side

items.note: The core strength pairing — squat + core (partner style)

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.detail: Dumbbell at chest, 3-sec descent, deep

exercises.name: Dead bug
exercises.detail: Opposite arm + leg, back flat — S holds a 2-3 lb dumbbell

exercises.name: TRX assisted deep squat hold
exercises.detail: Deepest squat possible, heels flat

exercises.name: Side plank
exercises.detail: From knee or full

exercises.name: Pigeon pose
exercises.detail: 6 slow breaths per side, both boys

weeks.name: Basement Day B — Position Skills + Mobility
weeks.tip: Tape lines on the basement floor for shuffles and dot hops — quiet, low-impact, works great inside.

exercises.name: Jumping jacks + hip circles + ankle rolls
exercises.detail: 1 min each — jacks can be low-impact (step instead of jump) if floor noise matters

g.name: React & drop
g.detail: Dad calls left/right — lunge into wide lateral stance, low and stable. No knee-drops on hard floor.

s.name: Crossover step-overs
s.detail: Cross over a line, push laterally off the back foot

exercises.name: Dot hops (4-square)
exercises.detail: 4 taped dots, all patterns — quiet on a mat

exercises.name: Mirror drill
exercises.detail: Brothers mirror each other's shuffles, low stance

exercises.name: Hip 90/90 stretch
exercises.detail: Lean over the front shin

exercises.name: Butterfly stretch + hamstring + 5 deep breaths
exercises.detail: Slow and easy

weeks.name: Field Day — Speed Development
weeks.tip: This is where yard lines pay off — every distance below is exact, no pacing-out needed.
weeks.swap: Rained out? Run a Basement Day instead, and shift this session to the next good-weather day — even mid-week is fine.

exercises.name: Skipping + inchworm walk-out
exercises.detail: 2 min skip, 5 inchworms

exercises.name: A-skips
exercises.detail: High knee, opposite arm, snap the foot down

exercises.name: Falling starts
exercises.detail: Lean until balance breaks, then go

items.note: Direction change on real yard markings

exercises.name: 5-10-5 shuttle run
exercises.detail: Use the 5-yard lines — time every rep, separate records

exercises.name: Crab walk race
exercises.detail: Sideways 5m and back

items.note: Straight speed + lateral power on grass

exercises.name: Driveway sprints
exercises.detail: Falling start into 15m max sprint (use yard lines), full walk-back rest

exercises.name: Lateral bounds
exercises.detail: Stick each landing 1 sec

exercises.name: Hip flexor stretch + lying hamstring
exercises.detail: 45 sec each side, on a towel on the grass

weeks.name: Basement Day A — Strength + Push/Pull
weeks.tip: Loads climb here for the 12yo. The dumbbells are the whole point of this session.

exercises.name: Bear crawl + thoracic rotations
exercises.detail: 3 lengths, 10 rotations per side

items.note: The core strength pairing — squat + hinge

exercises.name: Goblet squat
exercises.detail: Dumbbell at chest, 3-sec descent, deep

exercises.name: Romanian deadlift
exercises.detail: Hinge, flat back, feel the hamstrings

g.name: Swiss ball jackknife
g.detail: Plank, shins on ball, knees to chest

s.name: Swiss ball Russian twist
s.detail: Bridge on the ball, rotate a 5 lb dumbbell side to side

exercises.name: TRX row
exercises.detail: Plank-straight, chest to hands — S walks feet forward for harder angle

exercises.name: Pigeon pose
exercises.detail: 6 slow breaths per side — both boys, every time

exercises.name: Frog stretch + seated glute stretch
exercises.detail: 60 sec frog, 45 sec glute per side

weeks.name: Basement Day B — Position Skills + Jumps
weeks.focus: Low-impact jumps on a mat + reaction
weeks.tip: Use a foam/rubber mat for squat jumps here — keeps it quiet and joint-friendly.

exercises.name: Jump rope (or pretend) + hip airplanes
exercises.detail: 2 min rope, 6 airplanes per side

items.note: Low-impact power on the mat + upper push

exercises.name: Squat jumps
exercises.detail: On the foam mat — explode up, land soft, pause at bottom

exercises.name: Push-up handles variation
exercises.detail: Push-up, hop hands sideways, push-up

g.name: Drop-step to save
g.detail: Goalie stance, Dad points — drop step, wide lunge, hold 2 sec. No knee-drops indoors.

s.name: Crossover step-overs
s.detail: Cross over a line, push laterally off the back foot

exercises.name: Reaction ball drops
exercises.detail: Catch before the 2nd bounce — small movements, low ceiling friendly

exercises.name: Doorframe chest stretch + seated forward fold
exercises.detail: 45 sec each

weeks.name: Field Day — Top Speed + Game Speed
weeks.focus: Flying sprints, star drill, combo jumps
weeks.tip: Pre-season tempo. Time everything — Test day #2 numbers are the targets for today's reps.
weeks.swap: Rained out? Run a Basement Day, shift this to the next good-weather day. By Phase 3 the boys know the drills well enough that a day's delay costs nothing.

exercises.name: Dynamic circuit
exercises.detail: High knees, butt kicks, lateral shuffle — 20 sec each × 3

exercises.name: A-skips
exercises.detail: 2 × 10m, full mechanics focus

items.note: Explosive chain + single-leg strength — grass is ideal

exercises.name: Combo: squat jump + lateral bound
exercises.detail: Jump, land, immediately bound sideways, stick it

exercises.name: Single-leg squat to box
exercises.detail: Use a soccer ball or low cone target for the free heel to tap

exercises.name: Flying sprints
exercises.detail: Jog 5m in, max speed 10m, coast — full rest

exercises.name: 5-cone star drill
exercises.detail: Centre + 4 corners, sprint out and back — timed

exercises.name: Resisted sprint
exercises.detail: TRX loose around the waist, Dad anchors — sprint out 5m

exercises.name: Hip flow: 90/90 → pigeon → figure-4 → frog
exercises.detail: 45 sec each position, towel on grass

weeks.name: Basement Day A — Heavy Strength
weeks.tip: If the 12yo is breezing through, nudge weight up — only after a week of perfect form at the current load.

exercises.name: Full dynamic flow
exercises.detail: Inchworms → bear crawl → world's greatest stretch → leg swings

items.note: The main lift pairing — squat + pull

exercises.name: Front squat
exercises.detail: Weight at chest, elbows up, deep and controlled

exercises.name: Single-arm dumbbell row
exercises.detail: Knee on bench, row to the hip

exercises.name: TRX fallout
exercises.detail: Rigid body, arms overhead — keep range short under a low ceiling

exercises.name: Lateral lunge
exercises.detail: Wide step, sit into the hip — S holds 5-10 lb at chest

s.name: Standing dumbbell chop
s.detail: Rotate dumbbell hip to chest height — stop below ceiling line

exercises.name: Step-up with knee drive
exercises.detail: Heel drive, knee high, pause

exercises.name: Pigeon pose
exercises.detail: 6 slow breaths per side, both boys

exercises.name: Full stretch sequence
exercises.detail: Pigeon → frog → thoracic rotation → child's pose

weeks.name: Basement Day B — Quickness + Hands
weeks.tip: End with the mini shootout against a wall whenever you can — it's the best position-specific finisher either of them gets.

exercises.name: Tape agility ladder + shoulder circuit
exercises.detail: High knees then lateral through 10 rungs; arm circles + doorframe stretch

exercises.name: Speed squat
exercises.detail: Fast down, explosive up, no pause — quiet, no jump

exercises.name: Plank shoulder taps
exercises.detail: Hips dead still

g.name: Wall ball reaction
g.detail: Lacrosse ball, varied angles — goalie hands

s.name: Standing dumbbell chop
s.detail: Rotate dumbbell hip to chest height — stop below ceiling line

exercises.name: TRX single-leg lunge
exercises.detail: Back foot in strap, pause at the bottom — both
